---
title: "Data.rmd"
output: html_document
date: "2026-03-30"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
#https://api.eia.gov/v2/electricity/retail-sales/data/?frequency=monthly&data[0]=price&data[1]=sales&start=2020-01&end=2025-12&sort[0][column]=period&sort[0][direction]=desc&offset=0&length=5000

#needed cause trying to download everything at once doesn't work, so we have to split it up into multiple files.
#for (year in 2018:2025) {
  #linkTemplate = "https://api.eia.gov/v2/electricity/retail-sales/data/?frequency=monthly&data[0]=price&data[1]=sales&sort[0][column]=period&sort[0][direction]=desc&offset=0&length=5000&api_key=gZv331F0xrjrk5bsB2nw0Y4ldiBDmnsTJXYUSMrY";
  
  #downloadString = paste0(linkTemplate, "&start=", year, "-01", "&end=", year, "-12");
  #download.file(downloadString, paste0("electricity_data_", year, ".json"));
#}

#all we need is period, stateid (filter out entries that are not "ALL"), price, sales, price-units, sales-units

#parse each JSON, write data back as one .csv
files = list.files(pattern = "\\.json$");
files;

library(jsonlite);
library(dplyr);


#test = fromJSON(files[1], flatten = TRUE)
#str(test)

#this is weird lambda syntax for combining all data files
data = files %>% 
  lapply(function(f) {
    json = fromJSON(f, flatten = TRUE)
    json$response$data
  }) %>%
  bind_rows();

data;

#next time, remove unnecessary columns, then serialize data frame into .csv
```
